novel_features.name: Smart watch with diff types
novel_features.description: Periodic poll for a saved search that emits NEW, PRICE-DROP, and SEED events instead of any-listing-I-haven't-seen — so true new listings stand out from edits and reposts. Cross-city repost detection lives in the separate `reposts` command; cross-city dup detection lives in `dupe-cluster`.
novel_features.rationale: Requires per-watch seen-listing history plus a snapshot table of price/title diffs over time. Native CL email alerts and CPlus push notifications can't distinguish edits or reposts from new posts.
novel_features.example: craigslist-pp-cli watch save apartments --query 1BR --negate furnished,sublet --sites sfbay --category apa --max-price 2500 && craigslist-pp-cli watch run apartments --seed-only && craigslist-pp-cli watch run apartments --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this over a generic search call when an agent is alerting a user — only true-new listings are worth pinging on, and the typed diff event tells the agent how to phrase the alert.

novel_features.name: Streaming watch tail
novel_features.description: Long-running tail that prints one JSON event per new diff result so an agent or shell pipeline can react in real time. Emits NEW and PRICE-DROP events.
novel_features.rationale: Without local snapshot history there is nothing to diff against; without a saved-search definition there is no scope to poll. Both come from the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When you want a continuous stream of new-listing events to pipe into a Slack/email sender, jq filter, or downstream agent loop.

novel_features.name: Price drift history
novel_features.description: Show the price timeline for a single listing across every snapshot we've captured.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the local listing_snapshot table that no other CL tool builds. Visualping and PageCrawl monetize this; this command makes it free.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents negotiating on behalf of a buyer can quote concrete price-drop history — "this listing was $50, now $35, posted 14 days ago."

novel_features.name: Cross-city duplicate clusters
novel_features.description: Find listings whose body fingerprint and image hash match across cities. Surfaces cross-city scams and aggregator reposts.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-city dedup requires listings from multiple cities in one store. The body shingle + image hash is computed locally on synced data.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before driving across town to view a rental — a cluster of 6+ cities for one apartment is almost always a scam.

novel_features.name: Scam score
novel_features.description: Rule-based 0-100 score for a listing using brand-new-account, below-median-price, wire-transfer keywords, and cross-city duplicate signals.
novel_features.rationale: Requires the local listing store (median price), the body text from rapi, and the duplicate-cluster output. Mechanical rules; no LLM needed.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents triaging "is this listing legit" questions get an actionable number plus the per-rule contributions, instead of a vibes-based answer.

novel_features.name: Median price across the local market
novel_features.description: p25/p50/p75 of prices for a query, optionally over a time window or split by city.
novel_features.rationale: Pure SQL aggregation over the local listing + listing_snapshot tables. CL has no native aggregation endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs a fair-price benchmark before suggesting an offer, or when a reseller is sizing up a market.

novel_features.name: Negative-keyword smart search
novel_features.description: Search with NOT-keyword filtering that Craigslist's own search doesn't support natively.
novel_features.rationale: Calls sapi with the positive query, then applies SQLite FTS5 NOT filtering against the local body text. Works because the local store mirrors what was returned.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Apartment-hunting and job-search personas live and die by exclusion terms; this is the difference between scanning 50 results and scanning 5.

novel_features.name: One-shot what's new since
novel_features.description: Ad-hoc "what's new in this category in this city since X duration ago" without setting up a saved search.
novel_features.rationale: Reads sitemap-by-date for the target days (the RSS replacement), fetches detail only for listings not already in the local store, and emits the diff.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The first command an agent should run for "what hit while I was sleeping" — no per-watch setup, no state, just a window.

novel_features.name: Reposts radar
novel_features.description: Find listings that have been reposted N or more times in the last X days, by body fingerprint clustering.
novel_features.rationale: Body-hash clustering across listing_snapshot rows reveals reposts disguised as new listings. The local snapshot table is the only data source where this is computable.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reposts signal motivated sellers (negotiation leverage) or spam flooders (skip). Telling them apart is the value.

novel_features.name: City-by-city heat map
novel_features.description: Across cities, count fresh listings per category over a window. Surfaces which markets are hot for what.
novel_features.rationale: Reads sitemap-by-date counts per (city, category) without fetching listing detail. Pure metadata aggregation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent is hunting cross-city for a specific item, knowing which 3 cities have the most fresh activity tells it where to look first.

narrative.headline: The local-first Craigslist watcher and triage tool that knows what's a repost, what's a scam, and what just dropped in price.
narrative.value_prop: craigslist-pp-cli wraps Craigslist's own undocumented JSON endpoints (sapi, rapi, reference) — the same ones the Craigslist mobile app uses — and layers a local SQLite snapshot history on top. That powers cross-city search, saved-search alerts that distinguish true new listings from edits and reposts, price-drift detection, and cross-city duplicate / scam scoring. Free, scriptable, no PII selling, no proxies, no API key.
narrative.auth_narrative: No authentication required. Craigslist's read endpoints (sapi.craigslist.org/web/v8, rapi.craigslist.org/web/v8, reference.craigslist.org) are public. The CLI sets a polite User-Agent and reuses the cl_b cookie Craigslist auto-issues. Posting and account management are intentionally out of scope for v1.

quickstart.comment: Pull the 178-category and 707-area reference taxonomy into the local store; refreshed at most every 30 days per Craigslist's own Cache-Control.
Single-city search with a price cap; default human table output.
Cross-city hunt for a rare item — fans out parallel sapi calls, source-attributes results.

quickstart.command: craigslist-pp-cli watch save apartments --query 1BR --negate furnished,sublet --sites sfbay --category apa --max-price 2500
quickstart.comment: Save a smart search with negative keywords Craigslist's own search doesn't support.
Stream new-listing events as JSON lines: [NEW] / [PRICE-DROP].
Rule-based scam triage on a listing — useful before driving to view a too-good rental.

troubleshoots.symptom: HTTP 403 with body 'Your request has been blocked'
troubleshoots.fix: You hit Craigslist's anti-bot threshold. Wait 30 minutes, then resume with longer poll intervals (--interval 5m or higher). Avoid running parallel watches across more than 3-5 sites at once.

troubleshoots.symptom: Search returns 0 items but the website shows results
troubleshoots.fix: Confirm the site abbreviation with `craigslist-pp-cli areas list --grep <name>`; sapi rejects requests against unknown hostnames silently. Hostname is the short slug (sfbay, nyc, losangeles), not the city name.

troubleshoots.symptom: `watch run` keeps reporting the same listings as NEW
troubleshoots.fix: The local store is missing or got reset. Run `craigslist-pp-cli watch run <name>` once with `--seed-only` to populate the seen-listing baseline without alerting.

troubleshoots.symptom: RSS feeds 403 — "the URL has ?format=rss in it"
troubleshoots.fix: Craigslist hard-blocks RSS as of 2024. The CLI uses sapi/rapi/sitemap-by-date instead — never call CL's RSS URL directly.

narrative.when_to_use: Use craigslist-pp-cli when you need scriptable, local-first access to Craigslist listings — searching with filters Craigslist's own UI doesn't support (NOT-keyword), watching saved searches across multiple cities and getting alerted only on true new listings, doing price-drift or repost detection on housing, and triaging suspect rentals against cross-city duplicate signals. Best for read-heavy workflows: watching, exporting, comparing, scoring. Posting and account management are out of scope.

recipes.title: Hunt one item across the western US
recipes.explanation: Fans out across 8 west-coast sites in parallel and narrows the JSON to the shape an agent or jq pipeline needs.

recipes.title: Watch for a 1BR that isn't furnished or a sublet
recipes.command: craigslist-pp-cli watch save sf-1br --query '1BR' --sites sfbay --category apa --max-price 2800 --negate furnished,sublet,studio,airbnb && craigslist-pp-cli watch tail sf-1br --interval 5m --json
recipes.explanation: Saved search uses negative keywords Craigslist itself doesn't support; tail emits a stream of [NEW] / [PRICE-DROP] events.

recipes.title: Triage a too-good listing
recipes.explanation: Score returns the per-rule contributions; dupe-cluster shows whether the same listing exists in other cities.

recipes.title: Quote a fair-price range
recipes.explanation: p25/p50/p75 over the last 30 days, split by city, computed from the local listing_snapshot table.

recipes.title: What hit while I was asleep
recipes.explanation: Reads sitemap-by-date for the target window, fetches detail only for listings not already in the local store, emits the diff.
